agent_notes: Bexley has a strong independent micropub culture and several heritage/craft anchors, but the PAINTS backup rule is the main limiter: many good venues do not have a qualifying same-category backup within 10 minutes on foot.

Creative Walks are limited to Danson Park only: it is the only Bexley green space on the PAINTS v6 approved safety list. Foots Cray Meadows, Lesnes Abbey Woods and Hall Place gardens were not used for Creative Walks.
Books & Coffee is thin in-borough. The Exchange and Sidcup Storyteller qualify only as book/library/culture-plus-cafe hybrids, not classic independent bookshop cafes.
Opening hours, walk-in/group tolerance and private-hire conflicts should be host-confirmed before publishing; fields marked UNVERIFIED were not safely evidenced from live sources.

venues.opening_hours: CAMRA current listing: Monday closed; Tuesday-Thursday 3pm-10pm/10.30pm; Friday 2pm-10.30pm; Saturday 2pm-10.30pm; Sunday 2pm-8pm. Host must confirm holiday/private-hire exceptions.
venues.walk_in_policy: Micropub format indicates walk-ins; group of 8-12 should arrive off-peak or host should call ahead. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable for a conversational group off-peak; small-room micropub means avoid Friday/Saturday peak.
venues.capacity_notes: Small former shopfront micropub; seating may fragment at peak times.
venues.why_fits_paints: London's first permanent micropub has a strong local story, gravity cask ritual and a no-frills conversation-first feel. It is more characterful than a standard high-street pub and less obvious than central craft-beer picks.
venues.anecdote_story: The premises was formerly a corn chandlers and later an electrical retailer; the pub opened in March 2013 and helped define Bexley's micropub scene.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Gravity-dispensed cask ales, cider and changing real ale.
venues.safety_notes: High-street setting close to Welling station and bus routes; use lit high-street route after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk via Welling High Street/Bellegrove Road - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small-room capacity and peak football/weekend crowding; group tolerance not published.

venues.opening_hours: Venue/CAMRA: Monday closed; Tuesday-Wednesday 3.30pm-10pm; Thursday 3.30pm-10.30pm; Friday-Saturday 2pm-10.30pm; Sunday 2pm-8pm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins appear accepted; Instagram/website also invite booking enquiries. Group of 8-12 should call ahead. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better than many micropubs because it advertises a private event space, but PAINTS should avoid treating that as guaranteed without confirmation.
venues.why_fits_paints: A neighbourhood micropub with a cleaner, slightly more styled feel than many cask-led rooms. It works for show-and-tell because the drinks offer is independent and the space is intimate without feeling generic.
venues.anecdote_story: Local micropub in Falconwood Parade; recent social activity highlights open-mic/community use.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cask ales, beers, cocktails and wines.
venues.safety_notes: Falconwood Parade is suburban and close to rail; use main roads after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 9-10 min walk - verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Published walk-in policy for groups is not explicit; private hire could displace casual groups.

venues.opening_hours: Venue: Monday-Tuesday closed; Wednesday 5pm-9pm; Thursday 4pm-9.30pm; Friday 3pm-11pm; Saturday 2pm-11pm; Sunday 2pm-8pm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins likely accepted, but venue has booking/private-hire flow; group walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable only if off-peak or pre-cleared; compact micropub.
venues.capacity_notes: Small bar; children welcome before 6pm per venue page.
venues.why_fits_paints: Craft beer and cask in a small independent setting, with enough personality for a show-and-tell drinks format. It is operationally useful as part of the Welling backup triangle.
venues.anecdote_story: Sister venue to The Hangar Sidcup; the group operates two small neighbourhood bars in Bexley.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft beer, cask ales, cocktails, wine and spirits.
venues.safety_notes: Bellegrove Road/Welling station route is direct and lit; avoid late dispersal through side streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min walk - verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Short midweek hours and group booking ambiguity keep it Tier B.

venues.walk_in_policy: Micropub format suggests walk-ins; group of 8-12 must be confirmed because no group walk-in policy found.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable early evening but not peak weekend.
venues.capacity_notes: Micropub scale; recent refurb praised by venue testimonials.
venues.why_fits_paints: A well-loved local micropub with cask/keg focus and enough polish for strangers to settle in quickly. It is Tier B because no same-category backup was found within 10 minutes on foot.
venues.anecdote_story: Londonist profiled it as a Bexleyheath micropub gem; CAMRA lists it as individually leased and an Asset of Community Value.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cask and keg beer, plus wider micropub drinks.
venues.safety_notes: Suburban high-street route; station walk is longer than ideal after dark, so taxis/buses may be needed.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying alternative is The Long Haul at roughly 20+ min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Operational fragility from lack of close backup; group seating unverified.

venues.opening_hours: Venue/CAMRA: Monday closed; Tuesday-Wednesday 3pm-10pm; Thursday 1pm-10pm; Friday-Saturday 1pm-11pm; Sunday 2pm-8pm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins likely accepted; Instagram mentions DM/text to book. Group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: CAMRA describes compact interior plus some outside forecourt seating.
venues.why_fits_paints: A small Kentish-ale-led room in an unshowy shopping parade, exactly the kind of outer-London texture PAINTS should notice. Lack of a tight same-category backup prevents Tier A.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened in August 2020 in a former tattoo parlour and has Bexley CAMRA award signals.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ales, cider, wines and gin.
venues.safety_notes: Long Lane is lit and bus-served; station walk may be long late evening.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying alternatives are The Kentish Belle or Bird & Barrel beyond the strict walk radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: No close backup; group capacity and walk-in tolerance not published.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Barnehurst, 3 min walk from Barnehurst rail station
venues.opening_hours: Venue: Monday-Tuesday closed; Wednesday 5pm-9.30pm; Thursday 5pm-10pm; Friday 4pm-10pm; Saturday 2pm-10pm; Sunday 2pm-6pm. Holiday exceptions noted on venue page.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins likely accepted; group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best for a smaller PAINTS group or off-peak 8-10; 12 may be tight.
venues.why_fits_paints: Bexley Brewery's first licensed venue gives the event a clear maker/operator story. It has more producer identity than a generic pub, but backup scarcity caps the score.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened in 2018 as Bexley Brewery Ltd's first licensed venue, taking over a former tropical fish shop.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Bexley Brewery beers, cask/craft beer and pub drinks.
venues.safety_notes: Very close to Barnehurst station; use main road route.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying alternative is The Long Haul beyond the strict walk radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Short opening hours; no tight backup; venue has listed seasonal closure notices before.

venues.opening_hours: Venue/CAMRA: Monday closed, open on bank holidays; Tuesday-Thursday 1pm-10pm; Friday-Saturday 12pm-10.30pm; Sunday 12pm-5pm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins likely accepted; group of 8-12 not evidenced. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially suitable in afternoon; evening group capacity uncertain.
venues.capacity_notes: Micropub near River Cray; cask room with viewing window.
venues.why_fits_paints: A real-ale-focused Crayford room with local CAMRA credibility and a tactile cask-cellar detail. It is good PAINTS material but operationally isolated.
venues.anecdote_story: Bexley's second micropub, opened in September 2014, with Kentish brewer focus and Bexley CAMRA Pub of the Year 2019 claim.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ale and cider direct from cask, craft keg, Guinness and pub drinks.
venues.safety_notes: Use main Crayford station/high-street routes; riverside approaches after dark should be avoided unless host-checked.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying alternative is Bird & Barrel/Long Haul by transit, not walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: No tight backup; Sunday closes early; group walk-in not confirmed.

venues.opening_hours: Venue/CAMRA: Monday closed; Tuesday 4pm-10pm; Wednesday 3pm-10pm; Thursday 2/3pm-10pm; Friday-Saturday 2pm-10pm; Sunday 2pm-8pm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins likely accepted; group walk-in is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible off-peak, but group size not published.
venues.capacity_notes: Small micropub; dogs and kids welcome per Instagram.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Bexley village/Albany Park micropub gives useful geographic counterweight and a local-regulars feel. The PAINTS risk is that it is operationally alone rather than backed by a nearby sister option.
venues.anecdote_story: Local micropub with current live beer-board signals in the Bexley CAMRA network.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Ales, lagers, ciders, wines, prosecco, gins, spirits and cocktails.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet suburban parade; confirm late-evening onward transport.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying alternative is The Hangar Sidcup or The Hackney Carriage beyond radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: No nearby backup; limited independent press signal.

venues.opening_hours: Venue/Facebook: Monday closed; Tuesday-Thursday 3pm-10pm; Friday-Saturday 12pm-11pm; Sunday 12pm-8pm. Recent Facebook snippet also showed special same-day hours, so host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins likely accepted; group walk-in and 8-12 seating not published. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Micropub setting; best at early/quiet times.
venues.capacity_notes: Small independent pub on Station Road.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Sidcup micropub with a warm, ale-led room and enough neighbourhood specificity to feel discovered. It misses Tier A because the backup walk is just outside the strict comfort zone.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened on 19 August 2016 and described as Boyle's independent pub by CAMRA.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ale, cider and micropub drinks.
venues.safety_notes: Station Road is direct and transport-adjacent; confirm lighting/late route for solo attendees.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying alternative is The Hopper's Hut or The Hangar Sidcup just beyond strict radius depending route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Special-hour changes appear on social channels; group size unverified.

venues.opening_hours: Venue/CAMRA: Monday-Tuesday closed; Wednesday 5pm-9pm; Thursday 5pm-10pm; Friday 3pm-11pm; Saturday 2pm-11pm; Sunday 2pm-9pm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins likely accepted; Instagram also mentions closures/private-event style updates, so group walk-in must be confirmed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially suitable off-peak; 12 may be tight.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has a lovely local-history hook: the name refers to hop-picking huts and the Sidcup/Kentish beer landscape. It stays Tier B because backups and closure notices create operational fragility.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened in October 2016 in a former party-supply shop; founders Simon and Rebecca Tippett named it after local hop-farming history.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cask ale, cider and neighbourhood micropub drinks; vinyl/quizzes appear in social bio.
venues.safety_notes: Sidcup High Street is transport-adjacent and lit; confirm late exit route.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying alternative is The Hackney Carriage or The Hangar Sidcup beyond strict walking radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Closed Monday-Tuesday and short Wednesday; occasional closure/private-event signals on Instagram.

venues.opening_hours: Official Hall Place/My Farmers Market: first Sunday and third Saturday monthly, 10am-2pm; next listed date Sunday 5 July 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: N/A - open-access market; meeting point at Riverside Cafe/Garden Room alternative must be host-confirmed for group.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 as a roaming market day; meet outside the Visitor Centre courtyard.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor courtyard market; stallholder attendance varies.
venues.why_fits_paints: A producer-led market in a Tudor house courtyard gives Bexley a real PAINTS anchor: food, craft, heritage and easy conversation. It is not just a retail market; the Hall Place setting does the cultural work.
venues.anecdote_story: Hall Place is a Grade I Tudor house and garden; the farmers market was a Farm Retail Association Farmers Market of the Year finalist signal.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Locally sourced produce, handmade wooden items, fish, fruit/veg, small-batch gin/rum and changing stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime heritage-site setting with parking and accessible visitor facilities; avoid isolated riverside routes after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 15 min by bus/taxi rather than walk; acceptable only under market backup relaxed radius if transit is allowed and date aligns.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather exposure, variable stallholder attendance, and dogs not allowed on the Hall Place site except assistance dogs.

venues.opening_hours: Council/Enjoy Bexleyheath sources list Friday night market dates with food, local traders, live performance and artisan products. 2026 recurrence/date must be host-confirmed from official event listing before use.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a roaming matched group if hosted; crowd density may make introductions harder.
venues.why_fits_paints: It brings artisan products, street food, craft beer/cocktails and live performance into Bexleyheath's civic centre. It is Tier B because recurrence/year certainty and weather/crowd conditions need final checking.
venues.anecdote_story: Part of Bexley's High Streets for All night-time economy work, with up to forty unique traders cited in council material.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, artisan products, craft beer, cocktails, live entertainment.
venues.safety_notes: Central, busy, lit town-centre setting; crowd management and onward rail/bus routes should be host-planned.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 15 min by transit only; not same-day/evening equivalent, so host should treat as schedule backup rather than immediate weather fallback.
venues.risks_watchouts: Date recurrence may be seasonal; night crowd and weather exposure; not as curated as Hall Place.

venues.opening_hours: GoParks lists Danson Park as always open; Bexley/Hall-style park facilities and private events must be host-checked for the chosen route/day.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a hosted daytime group; use a defined route and avoid after-dark starts.
venues.capacity_notes: Large 75/78-hectare public park; choose high-footfall loop around lake, English Garden, Charter Oak and Danson House exterior.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday or Sunday late morning/early afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: Danson Park is the one Bexley Creative Walk that passes the PAINTS safety-list constraint. It offers a compact story arc: Georgian villa, restored landscape, lake/boathouse, and the Charter Oak civic story.
venues.anecdote_story: The park opened to the public in 1925 after Bexley Urban District Council acquired the estate; the Charter Oak marks the 1937 municipal charter story and appears in local civic identity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: 3-4 stops: Danson House exterior; lake/Boathouse; English/Rock/Peace Garden; Charter Oak.
venues.safety_notes: Confirmed on PAINTS v6 Safety-First Shortlist. Daytime only; avoid isolated edges and after-dark dispersal.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of lake/Boathouse start point, but operationally weak because public food service appears Sunday-carvery/private-event led.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather backup is imperfect; Boathouse public walk-in availability is not robust, and Danson House public access is Sunday-only via separate heritage hours.

venues.opening_hours: Official: gardens open daily from 9am; April-September summer closing around 9.30/10pm; Visitor Centre and Riverside Cafe from 9.30am-4.30pm; current events include Love Art and Learn Exhibition 18 June-15 July 2026, free entry.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins likely for free gallery/visitor centre exhibitions; tours require advance booking. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm for 8-12.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for daytime group if using free exhibition/gardens/cafe rather than booked house tour.
venues.capacity_notes: Visitor centre/gallery setting supports circulation; cafe group seating should be confirmed.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekday late morning or Sunday after market browsing.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Tudor house, riverside gardens and Stables Gallery give Bexley a credible museum/gallery day without central-London obviousness. It is Tier B because the current exhibition is local rather than press-validated within the last three months.
venues.anecdote_story: Hall Place is a historic house with wartime code-name Santa Fe tour material and public museum/garden programming.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Current/free Stables Gallery art exhibition, historic house tours, gardens, visitor-centre displays.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime visitor attraction with accessible facilities; avoid isolated riverside edges after dusk.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying museum/gallery backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying cultural alternative is Red House by transit/taxi, not walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Press criterion is weak; historic house tours require booking; cafe/group seating unverified.

venues.opening_hours: National Trust page lists July-December 2026 opening calendars and asks visitors to book a visit; tours are guided/pre-booked per ticketing and 'things to see' pages.
venues.walk_in_policy: Timed/guided visit requiring pre-booking; capped at Tier B under PAINTS Gate 5.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable only if PAINTS books as a group in advance; not a walk-in event.
venues.capacity_notes: Guided house tours around 45-60 minutes; no parking per ticket page.
venues.best_time_slot: Pre-booked Thursday-Friday late morning if calendar availability exists.
venues.why_fits_paints: The William Morris/Philip Webb Arts and Crafts origin story is one of Bexley's strongest cultural cards. Operationally it conflicts with PAINTS' walk-in model, so it is a high-taste but fragile Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: The only house designed, built and lived in by William Morris; a hub for Jane Morris, Philip Webb and the Pre-Raphaelite circle.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Arts and Crafts interiors, Morris/Webb/Burne-Jones context, guided house interpretation.
venues.safety_notes: Residential setting; plan arrivals/departures by taxi/bus/rail, especially after dark.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying museum/gallery backup found within 10 min - closest cultural alternative is Hall Place by taxi/transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Pre-booked guided tours cap the tier; current-exhibition requirement is not strongly met beyond permanent heritage interpretation.

venues.venue_name: The Exchange Erith - Garden Room Shop & Coffee Bar
venues.neighbourhood_station: Erith, 3 min walk from Erith rail station
venues.opening_hours: Official pages: Garden Room Shop & Coffee Bar generally Thursday-Saturday around 10/10.30am-4pm, with some event-day variations.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins for shop/coffee appear accepted; 8-12 seated for two hours is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable as a slow daytime cultural-coffee stop if seating is confirmed; not a classic bookshop cafe.
venues.capacity_notes: Old Library arts/craft venue with shop, coffee bar, garden and workshops.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is Bexley's best Books & Coffee hybrid because the coffee bar sits inside a Grade II old library/craft organisation and stocks books alongside craft/homewares. The book offer is not deep enough for Tier A, but the setting is deeply PAINTS-coded.
venues.anecdote_story: The Old Carnegie Library has been revived as a community-led craft and garden hub; the Sarah Price-designed garden adds a contemporary design story.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Coffee, handmade craft goods, homewares, books, garden supplies, gifts and Sarah Price garden access on selected days.
venues.safety_notes: Short walk from Erith station; use main station route and daytime starts.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying bookshop-cafe backup found within 10 min - closest cultural alternative is Erith Library/civic cafe context, but it does not clear PAINTS Books & Coffee.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying 10-min Books & Coffee backup found.
venues.risks_watchouts: Limited opening days/hours; seating/group tolerance and depth of book stock should be checked.

venues.opening_hours: Bexley Libraries says Sidcup Storyteller library and cafe opened in December 2022; current cafe/cinema hours must be confirmed on Sidcup Storyteller channels. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Library/cafe walk-ins likely; group of 8-12 for two hours is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially suitable in library/cafe setting, but not independent and may have cinema/coworking pressures.
venues.capacity_notes: Library, cafe, three-screen cinema and community space in one building.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekday late morning outside school/library peaks.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is a civic culture version of Books & Coffee: books, cafe and architecture in a revived high-street building. It is not a purist independent bookshop cafe, so it remains a cautious Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: The former Blockbuster site was transformed into a library/cafe/cinema; the Guardian noted its William Morris-meets-multiplex design energy.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Library books, coffee/cafe offer, cinema/community programming.
venues.safety_notes: Central Sidcup High Street location; use lit high-street/station routes.
venues.backup_venue: The Hopper's Hut as a strong quiet cultural-drinks alternative, not a category-perfect Books & Coffee backup.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 5 min walk, but category mismatch means primary remains Tier B.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not independent; cafe hours/current operation need confirmation; category purity is weak.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Attractive local green space, but not on the PAINTS v6 Premium or Safety-First Creative Walk list. Do not source for Creative Walks.
Strong heritage/nature story but absent from the PAINTS approved Creative Walk parks list; not used as route or backup.
Good Blackfen micropub story, but recent closure/reopening under new management makes operational stability too uncertain for this run; host should re-check after a few months of steady trading.
Good independent micropub, but its nearest qualifying same-category backup appears outside the strict 10-minute walk radius and the Welling branch offers stronger backup geometry.
Useful near Danson Park, but CAMRA identifies it as Stonegate. Chain/operator profile fails PAINTS independent drinks filter.
Good lake setting but public offer appears Sunday carvery/private-event led, not Books & Coffee, and walk-in/group cafe operation is not robustly evidenced.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Broadway Shopping Centre / Festive Market Stalls
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Shopping-centre setting and seasonal retail framing are too mall/templated for PAINTS; Bexleyheath Night Markets are the stronger town-centre market option.
Outstanding industrial heritage but opening is event/open-day dependent and not a good walk-in 8-12 museum/gallery day without advance scheduling; backup geometry is poor.
Bookshop chain and shopping-centre/high-street retail feel fail PAINTS independent/character filter; no substantive independent cafe pairing evidenced.
